title: Match the build target to the runtime
description: Pick the compilation target (browser unknown vs WASI vs emscripten) for where the module runs so its imports are satisfiable.
body: Use when setting up or fixing a WASM build. Do not build for one runtime and load in another, because a wasm32-wasi module expects WASI syscall imports a browser cannot provide, while a wasm32-unknown-unknown module built for the web expects JS glue. Actions: choose wasm32-unknown-unknown with wasm-bindgen/wasm-pack for browsers, wasm32-wasi for server/WASI hosts, and emscripten when you need its POSIX/JS emulation; confirm the toolchain emits the bindings the loader expects. Validation signals include the module instantiating without missing-import errors and its imports matching what the host supplies. Risk boundary: shipping a WASI-targeted module to the browser (or vice versa) is a build-config defect; verify against the actual runtime before release.
